title: Contributing to rasterpic
text: outlines propose change rasterpic. detailed discussion contributing tidyverse packages, please see development contributing guide code review principles.

title: Contributing to rasterpic
text: can fix typos, spelling mistakes, grammatical errors documentation directly using GitHub web interface, long changes made source file. generally means ’ll need edit roxygen2 comments .R, .Rd file. can find .R file generates .Rd reading comment first line.

title: Contributing to rasterpic
text: want make bigger change, ’s good idea first file issue make sure someone team agrees ’s needed. ’ve found bug, please file issue illustrates bug minimal reprex (also help write unit test, needed). See guide create great issue advice.

title: Contributing to rasterpic
text: Fork package clone onto computer. haven’t done , recommend using usethis::create_from_github("dieghernan/rasterpic", fork = TRUE). Install development dependencies devtools::install_dev_deps(), make sure package passes R CMD check running devtools::check(). R CMD check doesn’t pass cleanly, ’s good idea ask help continuing. Create Git branch pull request (PR). recommend using usethis::pr_init("brief-description--change"). Make changes, commit git, create PR running usethis::pr_push(), following prompts browser. title PR briefly describe change. body PR contain Fixes #issue-number. user-facing changes, add bullet top NEWS.md (.e. just first header). Follow style described https://style.tidyverse.org/news.html.

title: Contributing to rasterpic
text: New code follow tidyverse style guide. can use Air apply style, please don’t restyle code nothing PR. use roxygen2, Markdown syntax, documentation. use testthat unit tests. Contributions test cases included easier accept.

title: Contributing to rasterpic
text: contributing guide adapted tidyverse contributing guide available https://tidyverse.tidyverse.org/CONTRIBUTING.html.

title: Plotting SpatRaster objects
text: straightforward option use base plot() methods provided terra package (terra::plotRGB()): Figure 1: Plot terra package
code: library(rasterpic) library(terra)  # Use the flag of the United Kingdom. img <- system.file("img/UK_flag.png",   package = "rasterpic" )  uk <- sf::st_read(   system.file("gpkg/UK.gpkg",     package = "rasterpic"   ),   quiet = TRUE )  uk_img <- rasterpic_img(uk, img, mask = TRUE, inverse = TRUE) plotRGB(uk_img)

title: Plotting SpatRaster objects
text: tidyterra package provides plotting support terra SpatRaster objects ggplot2: Figure 2: Plot tidyterra package

title: Plotting SpatRaster objects
text: tmap package can also create maps SpatRaster objects: Figure 3: Plot tmap package

title: Plotting SpatRaster objects
text: mapsf package can also plot SpatRaster objects: Figure 4: Plot mapsf package
code: library(mapsf)  mf_raster(uk_img) mf_scale()  mf_inset_on(x = "worldmap", pos = "topright") mf_worldmap(uk) mf_inset_off()

title: Plotting SpatRaster objects
text: maptiles package can download map tiles different providers. also provides function plotting terra SpatRaster objects: Figure 5: Plot maptiles package
code: library(maptiles)  other_tile <- get_tiles(uk, crop = TRUE, zoom = 6)  other_tile_crop <- terra::crop(other_tile, uk_img)  plot_tiles(other_tile_crop) plot_tiles(uk_img, add = TRUE)

title: Plotting SpatRaster objects
text: Tennekes M (2018). “tmap: Thematic Maps R.” Journal Statistical Software, 84(6), 1–39. doi:10.18637/jss.v084.i06. Giraud T (2026). mapsf: Thematic Cartography. doi:10.32614/CRAN.package.mapsf. Hernangómez D (2023). “Using tidyverse terra objects: tidyterra package.” Journal Open Source Software, 8(91), 5751. ISSN 2475-9066. doi:10.21105/joss.05751. https://doi.org/10.21105/joss.05751. Hijmans R, Brown , Barbosa M (2026). terra: Spatial Data Analysis. R package version 1.9-27, https://rspatial.org/. Wickham H (2016). ggplot2: Elegant Graphics Data Analysis. Springer-Verlag New York. ISBN 978-3-319-24277-4. https://ggplot2.tidyverse.org.

title: Get started
text: example uses shape Austria: Figure 1: Raster map geotagged coordinates Austria
code: library(sf) library(terra) library(rasterpic)  # Load plotting packages. library(tidyterra) library(ggplot2)  # Set the spatial object and image. x <- read_sf(system.file("gpkg/austria.gpkg", package = "rasterpic")) img <- system.file("img/vertical.png", package = "rasterpic")  # Create the raster. default <- rasterpic_img(x, img)  autoplot(default) +   geom_sf(data = x)

title: Get started
text: rasterpic_img() provides several options expansion, alignment, cropping masking. option expands raster extent beyond spatial object: Figure 2: Example image expansion Choose alignment image within spatial extent: Figure 3: Example image alignment Crop raster mask object shape: Figure 4: Example masked image Figure 5: Example inverse masked image
code: expand <- rasterpic_img(x, img, expand = 1)  autoplot(expand) +   geom_sf(data = x) bottom <- rasterpic_img(x, img, valign = 0)  autoplot(bottom) +   geom_sf(data = x) mask <- rasterpic_img(x, img, crop = TRUE, mask = TRUE)  autoplot(mask)  maskinverse <- rasterpic_img(x, img, crop = TRUE, mask = TRUE, inverse = TRUE)  autoplot(maskinverse)

title: Get started
text: option expands raster extent beyond spatial object: Figure 2: Example image expansion
code: expand <- rasterpic_img(x, img, expand = 1)  autoplot(expand) +   geom_sf(data = x)

title: Get started
text: Choose alignment image within spatial extent: Figure 3: Example image alignment
code: bottom <- rasterpic_img(x, img, valign = 0)  autoplot(bottom) +   geom_sf(data = x)

title: Get started
text: Crop raster mask object shape: Figure 4: Example masked image Figure 5: Example inverse masked image
code: mask <- rasterpic_img(x, img, crop = TRUE, mask = TRUE)  autoplot(mask)  maskinverse <- rasterpic_img(x, img, crop = TRUE, mask = TRUE, inverse = TRUE)  autoplot(maskinverse)

title: Get started
text: rasterpic_img() supports following input classes: sf classes: sf, sfc, sfg bbox. terra classes: SpatRaster, SpatVector SpatExtent. stars classes: stars. numeric coordinate vector form c(xmin, ymin, xmax, ymax). rasterpic_img() S3 generic. methods extent-like inputs use object extent, vector methods can also mask image object shape.

title: Get started
text: rasterpic can parse following image formats: png files. jpeg/jpg files. tiff/tif files.

title: Authors and Citation
text: Diego Hernangómez. Author, maintainer, copyright holder.

title: Authors and Citation
text: Hernangómez D (2026). rasterpic: Convert Digital Images SpatRaster Objects. doi:10.32614/CRAN.package.rasterpic. https://dieghernan.github.io/rasterpic/.
code: @Manual{R-rasterpic,   title = {{rasterpic}: Convert Digital Images to {SpatRaster} Objects},   doi = {10.32614/CRAN.package.rasterpic},   author = {Diego Hernangómez},   year = {2026},   version = {0.5.0},   url = {https://dieghernan.github.io/rasterpic/},   abstract = {Create SpatRaster objects, as defined by the terra package, from digital images using a spatial object as a geographic reference. Supported inputs include objects from the sf, terra and stars packages. The main function is an S3 generic, so other packages can provide methods for additional spatial classes.}, }

what: rasterpic | Convert digital images into SpatRaster objects
title: rasterpic | Convert digital images into SpatRaster objects
text: rasterpic tiny package single goal: transform image terra SpatRaster object (see ?terra::SpatRaster).

title: rasterpic | Convert digital images into SpatRaster objects
text: Install rasterpic CRAN:

title: rasterpic | Convert digital images into SpatRaster objects
text: can create custom maps several spatial input classes: sf classes: sf, sfc, sfg bbox. terra classes: SpatRaster, SpatVector SpatExtent. stars classes: stars. numeric coordinate vector form c(xmin, ymin, xmax, ymax). main function, rasterpic_img(), S3 generic. methods extent-like inputs use object extent, vector methods can also mask image object shape. example using sf object:  can also adjust parameters modify alignment image respect object:
code: library(rasterpic) library(sf) library(terra)  # Use the flag of the United Kingdom. img <- system.file("img/UK_flag.png", package = "rasterpic") uk <- read_sf(system.file("gpkg/UK.gpkg", package = "rasterpic"))  class(uk) #> [1] "sf"         "tbl_df"     "tbl"        "data.frame"  # Rasterize the image. uk_flag <- rasterpic_img(uk, img)  uk_flag #> class       : SpatRaster #> size        : 400, 800, 3  (nrow, ncol, nlyr) #> resolution  : 5398.319, 5398.319  (x, y) #> extent      : -2542183, 1776472, 6430573, 8589900  (xmin, xmax, ymin, ymax) #> coord. ref. : WGS 84 / Pseudo-Mercator (EPSG:3857) #> source(s)   : memory #> colors rgb  : 1, 2, 3 #> names       :   r,   g,   b #> min values  :   0,  14,  35 #> max values  : 255, 255, 255  # Plot with ggplot2 and tidyterra. library(tidyterra) library(ggplot2)  autoplot(uk_flag) +   geom_sf(data = uk, color = alpha("blue", 0.5)) # Align, crop and mask. uk_flag2 <- rasterpic_img(uk, img, halign = 0.2, crop = TRUE, mask = TRUE)  autoplot(uk_flag2) +   geom_sf(data = uk, fill = NA)

what: Compute aspect ratio for spatial input — asp_ratio
title: Compute aspect ratio for spatial input — asp_ratio
text: Computes aspect ratio width divided height, columns divided rows.

title: Compute aspect ratio for spatial input — asp_ratio
Compute aspect ratio for spatial input — asp_ratio
text: x SpatRaster object, sf/sfc object numeric vector length 4 coordinates c(xmin, ymin, xmax, ymax), created sf::st_bbox().

title: Compute aspect ratio for spatial input — asp_ratio
text: numeric scalar giving aspect ratio.

title: Compute aspect ratio for spatial input — asp_ratio
code: # \donttest{ library(terra) #> terra 1.9.27  x <- rast(system.file("tiff/elev.tiff", package = "rasterpic")) plot(x)  asp_ratio(x) #> [1] 2.34375 # }

what: rasterpic: Convert Digital Images into 'SpatRaster' Objects — rasterpic-package
title: rasterpic: Convert Digital Images into 'SpatRaster' Objects — rasterpic-package
text: Create 'SpatRaster' objects, defined 'terra' package, digital images using spatial object geographic reference. Supported inputs include objects 'sf', 'terra' 'stars' packages. main function S3 generic, packages can provide methods additional spatial classes.

title: rasterpic: Convert Digital Images into 'SpatRaster' Objects — rasterpic-package
text: Maintainer: Diego Hernangómez diego.hernangomezherrero@gmail.com (ORCID) [copyright holder] Authors: Diego Hernangómez diego.hernangomezherrero@gmail.com (ORCID) [copyright holder]

what: Convert an image into a geotagged SpatRaster — rasterpic_img
title: Convert an image into a geotagged SpatRaster — rasterpic_img
text: Geotags image based coordinates spatial object. rasterpic_img() S3 generic. rasterpic provides methods following classes: SpatExtent SpatRaster SpatVector bbox default numeric sf sfc sfg stars

title: Convert an image into a geotagged SpatRaster — rasterpic_img
code: rasterpic_img(   x,   img,   halign = 0.5,   valign = 0.5,   expand = 0,   crop = FALSE,   ... )  # S3 method for class 'sf' rasterpic_img(   x,   img,   halign = 0.5,   valign = 0.5,   expand = 0,   crop = FALSE,   ...,   mask = FALSE,   inverse = FALSE )  # S3 method for class 'sfc' rasterpic_img(   x,   img,   halign = 0.5,   valign = 0.5,   expand = 0,   crop = FALSE,   ...,   mask = FALSE,   inverse = FALSE )  # S3 method for class 'sfg' rasterpic_img(   x,   img,   halign = 0.5,   valign = 0.5,   expand = 0,   crop = FALSE,   ...,   mask = FALSE,   inverse = FALSE,   crs = NULL )  # S3 method for class 'stars' rasterpic_img(   x,   img,   halign = 0.5,   valign = 0.5,   expand = 0,   crop = FALSE,   ... )  # S3 method for class 'bbox' rasterpic_img(   x,   img,   halign = 0.5,   valign = 0.5,   expand = 0,   crop = FALSE,   ...,   crs = NULL )  # S3 method for class 'numeric' rasterpic_img(   x,   img,   halign = 0.5,   valign = 0.5,   expand = 0,   crop = FALSE,   ...,   crs = NULL )  # S3 method for class 'SpatRaster' rasterpic_img(   x,   img,   halign = 0.5,   valign = 0.5,   expand = 0,   crop = FALSE,   ... )  # S3 method for class 'SpatVector' rasterpic_img(   x,   img,   halign = 0.5,   valign = 0.5,   expand = 0,   crop = FALSE,   ...,   mask = FALSE,   inverse = FALSE )  # S3 method for class 'SpatExtent' rasterpic_img(   x,   img,   halign = 0.5,   valign = 0.5,   expand = 0,   crop = FALSE,   ...,   crs = NULL )

title: Convert an image into a geotagged SpatRaster — rasterpic_img
text: x R object (see S3 methods). img image geotagged. can local file URL (e.g. "https://.imgur.com/6yHmlwT.jpeg"). Accepted extensions png, jpeg/jpg tiff/tif. halign number 0 1 giving horizontal alignment img relative x. 0 aligns x left edge, 1 aligns right edge 0.5 centers horizontally. valign number 0 1 giving vertical alignment img relative x. 0 aligns x bottom edge, 1 aligns top edge 0.5 centers vertically. expand expansion factor bounding box x. 0 means expansion added, 1 means bounding box expanded double original size. See Details. crop Logical. raster cropped (expanded) bounding box x? See Details. ... arguments passed methods. mask Logical, available vector methods. raster masked shape x? See Details. inverse Logical. effect mask = TRUE. TRUE, areas raster covered x masked. crs Character string describing CRS. parameter applies x SpatExtent, sfg, bbox numeric coordinate vector. See CRS section.

title: Convert an image into a geotagged SpatRaster — rasterpic_img
text: SpatRaster object (see terra::rast()) layer corresponds color channel img: img least 3 layers, result additional property setting layers 1 3 red, green blue channels names "r", "g" "b" alpha applicable. img already definition RGB values (may case tiff/tif files) result keep channel definition. resulting SpatRaster RGB specification explained terra::RGB().

title: Convert an image into a geotagged SpatRaster — rasterpic_img
text: vignette("rasterpic", package = "rasterpic") explains, examples, effect parameters halign, valign, expand, crop mask.

title: Convert an image into a geotagged SpatRaster — rasterpic_img
text: rasterpic supports following input classes: sf classes: sf, sfc, sfg bbox. terra classes: SpatRaster, SpatVector SpatExtent. stars classes: stars. numeric coordinate vector form c(xmin, ymin, xmax, ymax). packages can provide methods additional spatial classes. Methods extent-like inputs use object extent. Methods vector inputs can also mask image object shape.

title: Convert an image into a geotagged SpatRaster — rasterpic_img
text: function preserves CRS x applicable. optimal results, use geographic coordinates (longitude/latitude). crs can WKT format, "authority:number" code "EPSG:4326" PROJ-string format "+proj=utm +zone=12". can also retrieved : sf::st_crs(25830)$wkt. terra::crs(). tidyterra::pull_crs(). See Value Notes terra::crs().

title: Convert an image into a geotagged SpatRaster — rasterpic_img
code: # \donttest{ library(sf) #> Linking to GEOS 3.12.1, GDAL 3.8.4, PROJ 9.4.0; sf_use_s2() is TRUE library(terra) library(ggplot2) library(tidyterra) #>  #> Attaching package: ‘tidyterra’ #> The following object is masked from ‘package:stats’: #>  #>     filter  x_path <- system.file("gpkg/UK.gpkg", package = "rasterpic") x <- st_read(x_path, quiet = TRUE) img <- system.file("img/vertical.png", package = "rasterpic")  # Default configuration. ex1 <- rasterpic_img(x, img)  ex1 #> class       : SpatRaster #> size        : 333, 250, 3  (nrow, ncol, nlyr) #> resolution  : 6484.467, 6484.467  (x, y) #> extent      : -1193414, 427703.2, 6430573, 8589900  (xmin, xmax, ymin, ymax) #> coord. ref. : WGS 84 / Pseudo-Mercator (EPSG:3857) #> source(s)   : memory #> colors rgb  : 1, 2, 3 #> names       :   r,   g,   b #> min values  :  15,   8,   4 #> max values  : 254, 255, 254  autoplot(ex1) +   geom_sf(data = x, fill = NA, color = "white", linewidth = 0.5)   # Expand. ex2 <- rasterpic_img(x, img, expand = 0.5)  autoplot(ex2) +   geom_sf(data = x, fill = NA, color = "white", linewidth = 0.5)   # Align. ex3 <- rasterpic_img(x, img, halign = 0)  autoplot(ex3) +   geom_sf(data = x, fill = NA, color = "white", linewidth = 0.5)  labs(title = "Align") #> <ggplot2::labels> List of 1 #>  $ title: chr "Align"  # Crop. ex4 <- rasterpic_img(x, img, crop = TRUE)  autoplot(ex4) +   geom_sf(data = x, fill = NA, color = "white", linewidth = 0.5) +   labs(title = "Crop")   # Mask. ex5 <- rasterpic_img(x, img, mask = TRUE)  autoplot(ex5) +   geom_sf(data = x, fill = NA, color = "white", linewidth = 0.5) +   labs(title = "Mask")   # Inverse mask. ex6 <- rasterpic_img(x, img, mask = TRUE, inverse = TRUE)  autoplot(ex6) +   geom_sf(data = x, fill = NA, color = "white", linewidth = 0.5) +   labs(title = "Mask Inverse")   # Combine inverse masking and cropping. ex7 <- rasterpic_img(x, img, crop = TRUE, mask = TRUE, inverse = TRUE)  autoplot(ex7) +   geom_sf(data = x, fill = NA, color = "white", linewidth = 0.5) +   labs(title = "Combine")  # }

title: rasterpic 0.5.0
text: Package documentation reviewed updated AI-assisted editing. rasterpic_img() now S3 generic methods supported input classes (#39). rasterpic_img() now supports stars objects.

title: rasterpic 0.4.0
text: CRAN release: 2026-03-21 Migrated vignettes Quarto.

title: rasterpic 0.3.1
text: CRAN release: 2026-01-14 New logo. minimum required R version now 4.1.0. Minor internal documentation improvements.

title: rasterpic 0.3.0
text: CRAN release: 2025-03-19 output 3 layers, first three renamed "r", "g" "b". cases 4 layers , fourth layer renamed "alpha". allows compatibility tmap >= 4.0 avoids error ! [subset] select layer name unique.

title: rasterpic 0.2.6
text: CRAN release: 2024-12-17 Updated documentation.

title: rasterpic 0.2.5
text: CRAN release: 2024-04-12 Updated documentation. Made Title Description fields DESCRIPTION specific.

title: rasterpic 0.2.4
text: CRAN release: 2024-01-18 Ensured SpatVector objects always CRS. image fewer 3 channels, raster output RGB property (terra::.RGB() FALSE). expected corner case. image file (e.g., tiff) already RGB definition, preserve output. Updated documentation tests.

title: rasterpic 0.2.3
text: CRAN release: 2023-09-08 Made asp_ratio() internal function. Improved documentation tests.

title: rasterpic 0.2.2
text: CRAN release: 2023-04-18 Removed unused dependencies. Improved documentation. Fixed typos messages. Declared output RGB raster terra::RGB(). Added ggplot2 “Suggests”.

title: rasterpic 0.2.1
text: CRAN release: 2022-06-10 Added tidyterra “Suggests”.

title: rasterpic 0.2.0
text: CRAN release: 2022-02-18 terra: SpatVector, SpatExtent. sf: sfg, sf::st_bbox(). Numeric vectors c(xmin, ymin, xmax, ymax). img parameter rasterpic_img() now accepts image URLs. Added article pkgdown site. Fixed CRAN errors.

title: rasterpic 0.1.0
text: CRAN release: 2022-01-27 Initial release.
